tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal expected movement by end-Q1 2026, with a median of $67,421 (flat to spot) and a tight interquartile range of $66,544-$68,553. The relatively low volume of $1.6M across 22 markets suggests limited conviction, though the 10th-90th percentile range ($66,018-$69,528) implies modest upside bias.

Markets price modest upside into early April with a median of $67,514 (+0.2%) and substantially higher liquidity at $8.4M across 61 markets. The 90th percentile reaches $70,553 while the 10th percentile falls to $64,412, reflecting meaningful tail risk in both directions over the week ahead.

Predictions show a flat trajectory with median at $67,519 (+0.2% from spot) and a widening distribution ($63,745-$71,565 decile range). The near-identical median to April 1st suggests markets are pricing sideways consolidation with increasing uncertainty, evidenced by a 75th percentile of $69,510.

By April 3rd, median expectations remain stable at $67,532 (+0.2%) but tail risks expand materially, with the 90th percentile climbing to $72,718 and 10th percentile dropping to $62,667. The broadening distribution ($65,176-$69,902 interquartile) indicates growing event uncertainty as the date approaches.

Markets signal a potential inflection point, with median holding at $67,520 but the 90th percentile capping at exactly $70,000, suggesting concentrated upside resistance. The notably tight upper bound combined with asymmetric downside risk (10th percentile at $62,353) implies either an event resolution or measurement artifact at this tenor.

tenors.narrative: Markets expect minimal WTI movement by end-Q1 2026, with a median of $104/bbl (+0.7%) and tight consensus across 26 active contracts. The 10th-90th percentile range of $101-$109 reflects low volatility expectations, suggesting traders anticipate stable fundamentals over the near term.

April 2026 pricing shows significant upside bias with a median of $122/bbl (+18.7%), though low liquidity (single market, $81k volume) limits conviction. The wide $71-$150 range indicates substantial uncertainty, likely reflecting competing seasonal demand and supply risk scenarios.

Mid-year 2026 presents a bifurcated outlook: the median of $77/bbl (-25.1%) suggests a material pullback, yet the 75th-90th percentile ($150-$200) captures a tail risk scenario of supply disruption or demand surge. This extreme dispersion across 13 markets warrants caution given the illiquidity in longer-dated oil contracts.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect gold to appreciate modestly to $4,842 by end-Q1 2026 (+6.2%), with 50% probability of prices between $4,563-$5,121. The 17 active markets show relatively tight consensus, though upside tail risk extends to $5,289 (90th percentile), suggesting institutional positioning for potential macro uncertainty.

Market expectations diverge sharply by end-Q2 2026, with the median declining to $4,128 (-9.5% from spot) but displaying extreme dispersion—the 25th-75th range spans $3,927-$5,556. This wide distribution reflects genuine uncertainty around Fed policy and real rates, with meaningful tail risk in both directions extending to $3,806 downside and $6,182 upside.
